free_text: Neutron capture and gamma-ray spectra measurements in
resolved resonances for the odd Nd-isotopes
Time of flight, flight path = 60 m,
the energy resolution varied between 1.5 nsec/m and
were measured with a 30 cm3 Ge(Li)-detector in the
energy range from 4 eV to 320 eV and 660 eV.
The neutron energy spectrum was measured
with a B-10-slab viewed by a NaI(Tl) crystal.
Absolute calibration of the product detector
efficiency times neutron flux was done with the black
resonance technique, in the peak region of the low
lying resonances in Ag-107 and Ag-109.
corrections for Doppler- and resolution effects,
the influence of multiple scattering on the area is
Resonance area analysis yields (g * gamma-width) for
large resonances or (g * neutron-width) for small ones
No evidence for a spin dependence of gamma-width is
